rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK VS. ARMITAGE BLOCK, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 30, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver Hearing Required. Defendant to be prepared to discuss financial details regarding the property, including, but not limited to: the total amount of rental income received since 2016; the total amount of verifiable paid expenses for the necessary maintenance of the property since 2016; the total amount of outstanding debts associated with the property, e.g. vendor's and service provider's invoices; the total amount of outstanding taxes; current rents paid for each unit, etc. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY VS. BEATRICE GISIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To 1) Stay This Action During The Pendency Of Deft'S Wrongful Foreclosure Lawsuit And 2) Confirm Unnamed Tenants Will Not Be Wrongfully Evicted Without Due Process; Supporting Memo And Declaration Of Andrew Martin
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 30, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT BEATRICE GISIE Notice Of Motion To 1) Stay This Action During The Pendency Of Deft'S Wrongful Foreclosure Lawsuit And 2) Confirm Unnamed Tenants Will Not Be Wrongfully Evicted Without Due Process GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to stay the unlawful detainer action pending the resolution of the quiet title action is granted. For the duration of this stay Defendant, claiming ownership, shall maintain insurance on the property and shall make all the tax payments. If Plaintiff currently has no insurance on the property, Plaintiff shall obtain insurance by October 15, 2019 and file a declaration re: compliance by October 16, 2019. If there are any outstanding property taxes, the payments must be made by October 15, 2019 and a declaration of compliance must be filed by October 16, 2019. As payments for insurance and taxes become due during the stay, Plaintiff must make timely payments and file declaration of full compliance the next day thereafter. Furthermore, Defendant, if she continues to be a landlord for unnamed non-parties, must agree to indemnify the Plaintiff for any tenancy-related claims advanced by such tenants based on the events/condition of the property/etc. during the pendency of the stay. Defendant must maintain the premises in habitable conditions and comply with all the applicable laws regarding tenancies. Motion is otherwise denied. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that she has standing to advance arguments on behalf of unnamed individuals. =(501/REQ)
